mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are positive in >65% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances or the signal loses predictive power during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer spending strength, which boosts corporate revenue forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in >55% of cases or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which supports short-term equity price appreciation.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict positive 5-day returns during expansion regimes or Rank IC drops below 0.02 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 315.0, elevated inflation triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which increases borrowing costs and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered observations or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising nonfarm payrolls and declining unemployment rates confirms robust labor market tightening, which drives wage growth and consumer spending, resulting in positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction signal yields negative forward returns in >50% of cases or the joint effect is not statistically significant.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP reflects underlying economic expansion, which increases corporate profit margins and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in >55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5 percent signals a sudden supply-side contraction, which reduces output expectations and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >65% of triggered instances or the threshold condition fails to separate winning and losing periods.
